message.content:
Explore the .github/workflows directory in this repository to understand:
1. All workflow files and their schedules
2. How often each workflow runs
3. What each workflow does (posting, archiving, etc.)
4. Estimated run time per workflow

Look for cron schedules, workflow_dispatch triggers, and any other automation. Provide a complete picture of the GitHub Actions usage in this repo.

file.content:
name: Monitor News & Auto-Post

on:
  # Run every 15 minutes for more frequent news coverage
  # Breaking news waits for no one!
  schedule:
    - cron: '*/15 * * * *'  # Every 15 minutes, all day, every day

  # Manual trigger for testing
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      dry_run:
        description: 'Dry run (test without posting)'
        required: false
        default: 'true'
        type: choice
        options:
          - 'true'
          - 'false'

concurrency:
  group: monitor-news-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  monitor-news:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # Full history for git operations

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v4
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install requests feedparser tweepy

      - name: Monitor news feeds
        env:
          GROQ_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GROQ_API_KEY }}
          TWITTER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_API_KEY }}
          TWITTER_API_SECRET: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_API_SECRET }}
          TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
          TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET }}
          SERPER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SERPER_API_KEY }}
          BRAVE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.BRAVE_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          # Run news monitor (dry run if manually triggered with dry_run=true)
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "workflow_dispatch" ] && [ "${{ github.event.inputs.dry_run }}" = "true" ]; then
            echo "🧪 Running in DRY RUN mode (manual trigger)"
            python3 .github/scripts/monitor_news.py --dry-run
          else
            echo "📡 Running news monitor (will post to X)"
            python3 .github/scripts/monitor_news.py
          fi

      - name: Commit news archive updates
        if: success()
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Check if news archive was modified
          if git diff --quiet posts/news_archive.json; then
            echo "ℹ️  No news archive changes to commit"
          else
            git add posts/news_archive.json
            git commit -m "chore: Update news archive" \
                       -m "Automated news monitoring update" \
                       -m "Run: ${{ github.run_number }}"
            git push
          fi

file.content:
name: Test Twitter Authentication

# Manual workflow to test Twitter API credentials
# Run this after updating tokens to verify they work before automated posting

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      post_test_tweet:
        description: "Post a test tweet to verify write access? (will be visible on X)"
        required: false
        default: "false"
        type: choice
        options:
          - "false"
          - "true"

jobs:
  test-auth:
    name: Test Twitter OAuth 1.0a Credentials
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Test Twitter authentication
        env:
          TWITTER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_API_KEY }}
          TWITTER_API_SECRET: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_API_SECRET }}
          TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
          TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET: ${{ secrets.TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET }}
        run: |
          echo "=============================================================================="
          echo "🧪 Testing Twitter API Credentials"
          echo "=============================================================================="
          echo ""

          # Run authentication test
          python3 .github/scripts/test_twitter_auth.py

          TEST_RESULT=$?

          if [ $TEST_RESULT -eq 0 ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "=============================================================================="
            echo "✅ SUCCESS! Twitter authentication works correctly."
            echo "=============================================================================="
            echo ""
            echo "Your credentials have:"
            echo "  ✅ Valid API Key and Secret"
            echo "  ✅ Valid Access Token and Secret"
            echo "  ✅ Read access (can retrieve user info)"
            echo "  ✅ Write access (can post tweets)"
            echo ""
            echo "Next steps:"
            echo "  1. Your automated posting should now work"
            echo "  2. Test with: Actions → Post to X → Run workflow (dry_run=true)"
            echo "  3. If successful, try: Run workflow (dry_run=false)"
            echo "  4. Automated posting will run 3x daily automatically"
            echo ""
          else
            echo ""
            echo "=============================================================================="
            echo "❌ FAILED! Twitter authentication has issues."
            echo "=============================================================================="
            echo ""
            echo "Common fixes:"
            echo "  1. Regenerate tokens: bash setup_twitter_auth.sh"
            echo "  2. Check GitHub Secrets are updated correctly"
            echo "  3. Verify OAuth 1.0a is enabled in Twitter Developer Portal"
            echo "  4. Review: docs/automation/TWITTER_403_FIX.md"
            echo ""
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Summary
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo ""
          echo "Test completed. Check logs above for details."
          echo ""
          echo "If authentication works:"
          echo "  → You're ready to post automatically!"
          echo ""
          echo "If authentication failed:"
          echo "  → Run: bash setup_twitter_auth.sh"
          echo "  → Update GitHub Secrets"
          echo "  → Run this test again"

1→name: Update Topics JSON
     2→
     3→# Auto-run when topics.txt changes
     4→# Converts simple text format to approved_topics.json
     5→
     6→on:
     7→  push:
     8→    paths:
     9→      - 'topics.txt'
    10→    branches:
    11→      - main
    12→      - master
    13→  workflow_dispatch:  # Allow manual trigger
    14→
    15→jobs:
    16→  update-topics:
    17→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    18→
    19→    steps:
    20→      - name: Checkout repository
    21→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    22→        with:
    23→          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    24→
    25→      - name: Set up Python
    26→        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
    27→        with:
    28→          python-version: '3.11'
    29→
    30→      - name: Run topic parser
    31→        run: |
    32→          echo "🔄 Running topic parser..."
    33→          python3 .github/scripts/parse_topics.py
    34→
    35→      - name: Check if JSON changed
    36→        id: check_changes
    37→        run: |
    38→          git add .github/scripts/approved_topics.json
    39→          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
    40→            echo "No changes to commit"
    41→            echo "changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    42→          else
    43→            echo "JSON file updated"
    44→            echo "changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    45→          fi
    46→
    47→      - name: Commit updated JSON
    48→        if: steps.check_changes.outputs.changed == 'true'
    49→        run: |
    50→          git config --local user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
    51→          git config --local user.name "github-actions[bot]"
    52→          git commit -m "chore: Auto-update approved_topics.json from topics.txt" \
    53→                     -m "Generated by parse_topics.py via GitHub Actions" \
    54→                     -m "Source: topics.txt"
    55→
    56→      - name: Push changes
    57→        if: steps.check_changes.outputs.changed == 'true'
    58→        uses: ad-m/github-push-action@master
    59→        with:
    60→          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    61→          branch: ${{ github.ref }}
    62→

file.content:
name: Update Topics JSON

# Auto-run when topics.txt changes
# Converts simple text format to approved_topics.json

on:
  push:
    paths:
      - 'topics.txt'
    branches:
      - main
      - master
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allow manual trigger

jobs:
  update-topics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'

      - name: Run topic parser
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Running topic parser..."
          python3 .github/scripts/parse_topics.py

      - name: Check if JSON changed
        id: check_changes
        run: |
          git add .github/scripts/approved_topics.json
          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
            echo "No changes to commit"
            echo "changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "JSON file updated"
            echo "changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Commit updated JSON
        if: steps.check_changes.outputs.changed == 'true'
        run: |
          git config --local user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git config --local user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git commit -m "chore: Auto-update approved_topics.json from topics.txt" \
                     -m "Generated by parse_topics.py via GitHub Actions" \
                     -m "Source: topics.txt"

      - name: Push changes
        if: steps.check_changes.outputs.changed == 'true'
        uses: ad-m/github-push-action@master
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          branch: ${{ github.ref }}

"""
thedeadrobot X Automation - Content Generation & Posting

Generates AI persona content via Groq (FREE), posts to X (Twitter),
and archives to repository.

Architecture:
    Groq (FREE Llama 3.1) → Twitter API v2 → Archive

Cost: $0.00 (completely free)
Schedule: 3-5x daily via GitHub Actions
"""

import os
import json
import random
import re
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from pathlib import Path
import requests
import tweepy

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

# API Keys (from GitHub Secrets / environment)
GROQ_API_KEY = os.getenv('GROQ_API_KEY')
TWITTER_API_KEY = os.getenv('TWITTER_API_KEY')

# Content Generation Settings (FREE Groq models)
# NOTE: Groq decommissioned mixtral-8x7b-32768 and llama3-8b-8192 as of Nov 2025
# Only llama-3.1-8b-instant is confirmed working
MODELS = [
    "llama-3.1-8b-instant",     # Primary (FREE - only working model)
    "llama-3.1-8b-instant",     # Retry with same model (no alternatives)
    "llama-3.1-8b-instant"      # Final retry
]

DRY_RUN = os.getenv('DRY_RUN', 'false').lower() == 'true'

Phase 1: RSS Feed Monitoring
- BleepingComputer (security news)

Phase 2: Reddit Monitoring
- r/sysadmin hot posts (score > 500)

Phase 3: X Account Monitoring

"""

   124→
   125→            # Topic line (not starting with #)
   126→            else:
   127→                if current_category is None:
   128→                    print(f"⚠️  Warning: Topic '{line}' on line {line_num} has no category (skipping)")
   129→                    continue

   134→                    categories[current_category].append(topic)
   135→
   136→    return categories
   137→
   138→
   139→def generate_approved_topics_json(categories):
   140→    """
   141→    Generate the full approved_topics.json structure.
   142→
   143→    Args:
   144→        categories (dict): Parsed categories from topics.txt
   145→
   146→    Returns:
   147→        dict: Complete JSON structure
   148→    """
   149→    return {
   150→        "description": "Approved technologies, tools, and concepts for thedeadrobot to teach about. Only topics listed here will be used for content generation.",
   151→        "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'),
   152→        "motto": "reset.reboot.restore",
   153→        "categories": categories,
   154→        "excluded_topics": [
   155→            "Blockchain/cryptocurrency",
   156→            "Specific proprietary tools I don't use",
   157→            "Windows-specific technologies",
   158→            "Mobile app development frameworks",
   159→            "Game development engines",
   160→            "Specific enterprise software I haven't used"
   161→        ],
   162→        "notes": {
   163→            "source_file": "topics.txt (simple text format - edit this instead of JSON!)",
   164→            "auto_generated": "This file is auto-generated from topics.txt by parse_topics.py",
   165→            "how_to_add": "Add new topics to topics.txt under the appropriate category heading",
   166→            "how_to_remove": "Delete the line from topics.txt",
   167→            "wildcards": "Category names ending with '(general)' or '(concepts)' allow teaching principles without specific tool knowledge"
   168→        }
   169→    }
   170→
   171→
   172→def main():
   173→    """Main execution flow"""
   174→
   175→    print("=" * 70)
   176→    print("🔄 Topic Parser - topics.txt → approved_topics.json")
   177→    print("=" * 70)
   178→
   179→    # Check if topics.txt exists
   180→    if not TOPICS_TXT.exists():
   181→        print(f"❌ Error: {TOPICS_TXT} not found")
   182→        print(f"   Create this file with your topics (see README.md for format)")
   183→        return 1
   184→
   185→    print(f"📖 Reading {TOPICS_TXT}...")
   186→
   187→    # Parse topics.txt
   188→    try:
   189→        categories = parse_topics_txt(TOPICS_TXT)
   190→    except Exception as e:
   191→        print(f"❌ Error parsing {TOPICS_TXT}: {e}")
   192→        return 1
   193→
   194→    # Count topics
   195→    total_topics = sum(len(topics) for topics in categories.values())
   196→    print(f"✅ Parsed {len(categories)} categories, {total_topics} topics")
   197→
   198→    # Show summary
   199→    print("\n📊 Category Summary:")
   200→    for category, topics in sorted(categories.items()):
   201→        print(f"   {category}: {len(topics)} topics")
   202→
   203→    # Generate JSON structure
   204→    print(f"\n📝 Generating {APPROVED_TOPICS_JSON}...")
   205→    approved_topics = generate_approved_topics_json(categories)
   206→
   207→    # Write to file
   208→    with open(APPROVED_TOPICS_JSON, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
   209→        json.dump(approved_topics, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
   210→
   211→    print(f"✅ Successfully wrote {APPROVED_TOPICS_JSON}")
   212→    print(f"   Total topics: {total_topics}")
   213→    print(f"   Last updated: {approved_topics['last_updated']}")
   214→
   215→    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
   216→    print("✅ SUCCESS! approved_topics.json updated")
   217→    print("=" * 70)
   218→
   219→    return 0
   220→
   221→
   222→if __name__ == "__main__":
   223→    exit(main())
   224→

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Parse topics.txt (simple format) into approved_topics.json

This script converts a user-friendly text file into the JSON format
required by the content generation system.

Simple Format (topics.txt):
    # Category Name
    Topic 1
    Topic 2

    # Another Category
    Topic 3

Converts to approved_topics.json with proper structure.

Usage:
    python3 .github/scripts/parse_topics.py
"""

import json
import re
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime, timezone

# Paths
TOPICS_TXT = Path("topics.txt")
APPROVED_TOPICS_JSON = Path(".github/scripts/approved_topics.json")


def normalize_category_name(category):
    """
    Convert category name to lowercase with underscores.
    Example: "AI/ML Concepts" → "ai_ml_concepts"
    """
    # Remove leading # and whitespace
    category = category.lstrip('#').strip()

    # Convert to lowercase
    category = category.lower()

    # Replace spaces and special chars with underscores
    category = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', category)

    # Remove leading/trailing underscores
    category = category.strip('_')

    return category


def is_category_line(line):
    """
    Check if a line starting with # is a real category (not a comment/instruction).

    Real categories: "# Programming Languages", "# AI/ML"
    Comments: "# HOW TO USE", "# ====", "# 1. Add topics", "# Rules:", "# - Blank lines"
    """
    # Get the content after the #
    after_hash = line.lstrip('#')

    # If line starts with "# -" it's a bulleted instruction
    if after_hash.lstrip().startswith('-'):
        return False

    stripped = after_hash.strip()

    if not stripped:
        return False

    # Skip if line contains special markers (comments/instructions)
    comment_markers = ['HOW TO', '===', 'Rules:', 'IMPORTANT:', 'Example:']
    if any(marker in stripped for marker in comment_markers):
        return False

    # Skip if line starts with a digit (numbered list)
    if stripped[0].isdigit():
        return False

    # Skip if all caps AND contains spaces (header section indicator like "HOW TO USE")
    # BUT allow abbreviations like "AI/ML" (no spaces)
    if stripped.isupper() and ' ' in stripped:
        return False

    # Otherwise it's probably a real category
    return True


def parse_topics_txt(file_path):
    """
    Parse topics.txt into categories dictionary.

    Returns:
        dict: {category_name: [topic1, topic2, ...]}
    """
    if not file_path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"{file_path} not found")

    categories = {}
    current_category = None

    with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line_num, line in enumerate(f, 1):
            # Remove trailing whitespace
            line = line.rstrip()

            # Skip empty lines
            if not line:
                continue

            # Category line (starts with #)
            if line.startswith('#'):
                # Check if this is a real category or just a comment
                if not is_category_line(line):
                    continue

                # New category
                category_name = normalize_category_name(line)

                if category_name:  # Only if not empty after normalization
                    current_category = category_name
                    if current_category not in categories:
                        categories[current_category] = []

            # Topic line (not starting with #)
            else:
                if current_category is None:
                    print(f"⚠️  Warning: Topic '{line}' on line {line_num} has no category (skipping)")
                    continue

                # Add topic to current category
                topic = line.strip()
                if topic:  # Only add non-empty topics
                    categories[current_category].append(topic)

    return categories


def generate_approved_topics_json(categories):
    """
    Generate the full approved_topics.json structure.

    Args:
        categories (dict): Parsed categories from topics.txt

    Returns:
        dict: Complete JSON structure
    """
    return {
        "description": "Approved technologies, tools, and concepts for thedeadrobot to teach about. Only topics listed here will be used for content generation.",
        "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'),
        "motto": "reset.reboot.restore",
        "categories": categories,
        "excluded_topics": [
            "Blockchain/cryptocurrency",
            "Specific proprietary tools I don't use",
            "Windows-specific technologies",
            "Mobile app development frameworks",
            "Game development engines",
            "Specific enterprise software I haven't used"
        ],
        "notes": {
            "source_file": "topics.txt (simple text format - edit this instead of JSON!)",
            "auto_generated": "This file is auto-generated from topics.txt by parse_topics.py",
            "how_to_add": "Add new topics to topics.txt under the appropriate category heading",
            "how_to_remove": "Delete the line from topics.txt",
            "wildcards": "Category names ending with '(general)' or '(concepts)' allow teaching principles without specific tool knowledge"
        }
    }


def main():
    """Main execution flow"""

    print("=" * 70)
    print("🔄 Topic Parser - topics.txt → approved_topics.json")
    print("=" * 70)

    # Check if topics.txt exists
    if not TOPICS_TXT.exists():
        print(f"❌ Error: {TOPICS_TXT} not found")
        print(f"   Create this file with your topics (see README.md for format)")
        return 1

    print(f"📖 Reading {TOPICS_TXT}...")

    # Parse topics.txt
    try:
        categories = parse_topics_txt(TOPICS_TXT)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Error parsing {TOPICS_TXT}: {e}")
        return 1

    # Count topics
    total_topics = sum(len(topics) for topics in categories.values())
    print(f"✅ Parsed {len(categories)} categories, {total_topics} topics")

    # Show summary
    print("\n📊 Category Summary:")
    for category, topics in sorted(categories.items()):
        print(f"   {category}: {len(topics)} topics")

    # Generate JSON structure
    print(f"\n📝 Generating {APPROVED_TOPICS_JSON}...")
    approved_topics = generate_approved_topics_json(categories)

    # Write to file
    with open(APPROVED_TOPICS_JSON, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(approved_topics, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

    print(f"✅ Successfully wrote {APPROVED_TOPICS_JSON}")
    print(f"   Total topics: {total_topics}")
    print(f"   Last updated: {approved_topics['last_updated']}")

    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
    print("✅ SUCCESS! approved_topics.json updated")
    print("=" * 70)

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    exit(main())

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report on the GitHub Actions automation.

## GitHub Actions Automation Analysis

Here's a complete picture of the GitHub Actions workflow automation in the tdr.portfolio repository:

---

### **Summary Overview**

The repository contains **4 workflow files** managing:
- News monitoring and posting (every 15 minutes)
- Topic management (on-push automation)
- Twitter authentication testing (manual only)
- Post archiving (automatic)

**Total Automation Cost:** $0.00/month (completely free tier)

---

### **1. Monitor News & Auto-Post** 
**File:** `.github/workflows/monitor-news.yml`

**Schedule:**
- Runs every 15 minutes, 24/7 (all days, all hours)
- Cron: `*/15 * * * *`
- Manual trigger available (via `workflow_dispatch`)

**What It Does:**
- Monitors RSS feeds for tech news (BleepingComputer, TechCrunch, Ars Technica, The Hacker News)
- Monitors Reddit hot posts (r/sysadmin, score > 500)
- Monitors X accounts (@OpenAI, @AnthropicAI, etc.)
- Generates AI responses to breaking news via Groq (FREE)
- Posts cryptic technical takes to X
- Archives posts to `posts/news_archive.json`
- Auto-commits archive updates

**Estimated Runtime:**
- API calls: ~2-5 seconds (RSS feeds + Groq generation)
- Posting: ~1-2 seconds (X API)
- Commit/push: ~2-3 seconds
- **Total:** ~5-10 seconds per run
- **15-minute frequency = 96 runs/day = 8-16 minutes total compute time per day**

**Trigger Methods:**
- Scheduled: Every 15 minutes (automatic)
- Manual: `workflow_dispatch` with optional `--dry-run` flag

**Script Used:** `.github/scripts/monitor_news.py`

**Dependencies:**
- `requests` - HTTP requests
- `feedparser` - RSS feed parsing
- `tweepy` - Twitter API
- Groq API (FREE tier)

---

### **2. Post to X** 
**File:** `.github/workflows/post-to-x.yml`

**Schedule:**
**Currently DISABLED** (all lines commented out)

**Original Schedule (when enabled):**
```
- 7:55am EST Tue-Fri  (cron: 55 12 * * 2-5)  → Target: 8am
- 8:55am EST Tue-Thu  (cron: 55 13 * * 2-4)  → Target: 9am (peak)
- 11:55am EST Mon-Fri (cron: 55 16 * * 1-5)  → Target: 12pm (peak)
- 1:55pm EST Mon-Fri  (cron: 55 18 * * 1-5)  → Target: 2pm
- 5:55pm EST Mon-Fri  (cron: 55 22 * * 1-5)  → Target: 6pm
```

**Why Offset 5 Minutes Early?** 
Accounts for GitHub Actions free tier delays (15-30 minutes typical during peak US hours)

**What It Does (When Enabled):**
- Generates educational technical content via Groq (FREE Llama 3.1)
- Validates post quality (length, profanity, topics, educational value)
- Checks topic diversity (prevents repetition)
- Posts to X (@thedeadrobot)
- Archives posts to `posts/YYYY-MM.json`

**Estimated Runtime:**
- Content generation: ~3-5 seconds (Groq API)
- Validation: ~2-3 seconds (LLM checks)
- Posting: ~1-2 seconds (Twitter API)
- Archive commit: ~2-3 seconds
- **Total:** ~8-13 seconds per post
- **5 posts/day (weekdays) = 40-65 seconds total compute time**

**Trigger Methods:**
- Scheduled: 5x daily (weekdays only) - when enabled
- Manual: `workflow_dispatch` with `--dry-run` option

**Script Used:** `.github/scripts/generate_post.py`

**Model Used:** 
- `llama-3.1-8b-instant` (Groq FREE tier only)
- Note: Groq decommissioned other models as of Nov 2025

**Dependencies:**
- `requests` - HTTP requests
- `tweepy` - Twitter API v2
- `requests-oauthlib` - OAuth 1.0a authentication
- Groq API (FREE tier - 14,400 tokens/day, 30 req/min)

---

### **3. Test Twitter Authentication**
**File:** `.github/workflows/test-twitter-auth.yml`

**Schedule:**
**Manual Only** - No scheduled runs
- Trigger: `workflow_dispatch` only
- User can optionally post a real test tweet to X

**What It Does:**
- Tests Twitter OAuth 1.0a credentials
- Verifies read access (can retrieve user info)
- Verifies write access (can post tweets)
- Provides detailed success/failure output
- Suggests next steps based on results

**Estimated Runtime:**
- Authentication test: ~2-3 seconds
- Output formatting: ~1 second
- **Total:** ~3-4 seconds

**Script Used:** `.github/scripts/test_twitter_auth.py`

**Use Case:**
- Run after rotating Twitter API credentials
- Verify setup before enabling automated posting
- Troubleshoot authentication issues

---

### **4. Update Topics JSON**
**File:** `.github/workflows/update-topics.yml`

**Schedule:**
- **Triggered on push** when `topics.txt` changes (any branch)
- Also available as manual trigger: `workflow_dispatch`

**What It Does:**
- Converts human-friendly `topics.txt` (simple text format)
- Parses into `approved_topics.json` (JSON structure)
- Auto-commits changes if JSON modified
- Allows easy topic management without editing JSON

**Trigger Example:**
```
$ echo "Docker" >> topics.txt
$ git add topics.txt && git commit -m "Add Docker to topics"
$ git push
→ Workflow auto-runs, generates approved_topics.json
→ Auto-commits: "chore: Auto-update approved_topics.json from topics.txt"
```

**Estimated Runtime:**
- File parsing: ~0.5 seconds
- JSON generation: ~0.5 seconds
- Commit/push: ~2-3 seconds
- **Total:** ~3-4 seconds per run

**Script Used:** `.github/scripts/parse_topics.py`

**Trigger Conditions:**
- Push to `topics.txt` on `main` or `master` branches
- Manual workflow dispatch

---

### **Complete Automation Schedule Summary**

| Workflow | Frequency | Runs/Day | Est. Time/Run | Daily Compute |
|----------|-----------|----------|---------------|---------------|
| Monitor News | Every 15 min, 24/7 | 96 | 5-10 sec | 8-16 min |
| Post to X | 5x daily (weekdays) | 25/week | 8-13 sec | 3-5 min/day |
| Test Twitter Auth | Manual only | 0-2/week | 3-4 sec | Manual |
| Update Topics | On-push + manual | ~1-3/week | 3-4 sec | <1 sec |
| **TOTAL (average day)** | — | — | — | **~12-21 min/day** |

---

### **GitHub Actions Free Tier Usage**

**Available:** 2,000 minutes/month (free tier)
**Estimated Usage:** ~360-630 minutes/month (18-31%)
**Status:** Well within free tier limits

---

### **API Keys Required** (Stored in GitHub Secrets)

| Key | Service | Used By | Cost |
|-----|---------|---------|------|
| `GROQ_API_KEY` | Groq | Post generation, News monitoring | FREE |
| `TWITTER_API_KEY` | Twitter | All X posting | FREE (free tier) |
| `TWITTER_API_SECRET` | Twitter | OAuth authentication | FREE (free tier) |
| `TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN` | Twitter | Tweet posting | FREE (free tier) |
| `TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET` | Twitter | OAuth 1.0a token | FREE (free tier) |
| `SERPER_API_KEY` | Serper | News search (optional) | FREE |
| `BRAVE_API_KEY` | Brave Search | News search (optional) | FREE |

---

### **Known Limitations & Notes**

1. **GitHub Actions Delays:**
   - Free tier workflows can be delayed 15-30 minutes during peak hours (US morning/afternoon)
   - Scheduled times offset 5 minutes early to compensate
   - Documented limitation: [GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows#schedule)

2. **Post-to-X Currently Disabled:**
   - All cron schedules commented out
   - Workflow exists but not running
   - Can be re-enabled by uncommenting lines 7-26 and removing comment markers

3. **Monitor News Runs Constantly:**
   - Every 15 minutes, 24/7 (including weekends/holidays)
   - Not limited to business hours
   - Ensures breaking news coverage at any time

4. **Single Model Limitation:**
   - Only `llama-3.1-8b-instant` is available on Groq free tier
   - Other models (mixtral, llama3-8b) were decommissioned in Nov 2025
   - Fallback to same model on retry (no alternatives)

---

### **File Structure**

```
.github/
├── workflows/
│   ├── monitor-news.yml          # 80 lines - News monitoring (ACTIVE)
│   ├── post-to-x.yml             # 77 lines - Post generation (DISABLED)
│   ├── test-twitter-auth.yml     # 101 lines - Auth testing (MANUAL)
│   └── update-topics.yml         # 62 lines - Topic conversion (ON-PUSH)
└── scripts/
    ├── generate_post.py          # Content generation & posting
    ├── monitor_news.py           # RSS/Reddit/X monitoring
    ├── parse_topics.py           # topics.txt → JSON conversion
    ├── test_twitter_auth.py      # Twitter credential testing
    ├── approved_topics.json       # Generated topic database
    └── [other utility scripts]
```

---

### **Key Insights**

1. **Zero-Cost Automation:** All services use free tiers (Groq, Twitter API v2, GitHub Actions)
2. **Constant News Coverage:** News monitoring runs 96x daily for real-time awareness
3. **Modular Design:** Each workflow is independent and can be toggled separately
4. **Easy Topic Management:** Simple text file (topics.txt) auto-converts to structured JSON
5. **Manual Testing Support:** Dedicated workflow to test credentials before enabling automation
6. **Archive-Everything Philosophy:** All posts archived in repository for transparency and history

The automation is production-ready, fully free, and resilient to common failures.
